rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical 1,2-carbohydroxylation of arylalkenes via anodic oxidation, adding an alkynyl/alkenyl group (from organotrifluoroborate) and OH (from H2O) across the C=C bond
electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of arylalkene to a radical cation, which is trapped by the organotrifluoroborate; the resulting C-radical is further anodically oxidized to a benzylic cation that is trapped by H2O. At the cathode, H2O is reduced to H2 and HO-.

key_intermediates.name: benzylic C-centered radical (D) after concerted C-C bond formation/BF3 loss
benzylic carbocation (E) from anodic oxidation of D

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes the arylalkene (Ep/2 = 1.57 V vs SCE) to a radical cation, which is intercepted by the organotrifluoroborate through a concerted C-C bond formation/BF3 release, yielding a benzylic radical; this radical is further oxidized at the anode to a carbocation that is trapped by H2O, while at the cathode H2O is reduced to H2 + HO-.

probe_reagents.name: photoredox control (Acr+-Mes ClO4- / [Co] cat., blue LED)
probe_reagents.observation: 0% of carbohydroxylation product 3; instead 58% aldehyde 77 formed, showing distinct selectivity vs electrochemistry

probe_reagents.name: CAN (cerium ammonium nitrate, chemical oxidant)
probe_reagents.observation: 0% of 3; benzophenone formed (25%) at 42% conversion, indicating alkene preferentially reacts with H2O under homogeneous SET oxidation

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylpropene (trisubstituted alkene)
probe_reagents.observation: Gave 27% hydroxy-alkynylation 32 plus 51% vicinal diol 33, 10% epoxide, and 9% benzophenone, consistent with a benzylic cation intermediate trapped competitively by H2O

probe_reagents.name: DFT calculation on radical cation + alkenyltrifluoroborate
probe_reagents.observation: Low activation barrier for a concerted C-C bond formation/C-B cleavage, supporting concerted (not stepwise) coupling that preserves alkene stereochemistry

notes: Alternative stepwise mechanism (C-C bond formation followed by SET oxidation and C-B cleavage, as proposed for enamine radical cations with alkenyltrifluoroborates, ref 9c) was considered but ruled out because alkenyltrifluoroborate stereochemistry is fully retained in product, favoring a concerted C-C/C-B step. Selectivity for H2O over a second equivalent of organotrifluoroborate at the cation E is rationalized by steric hindrance.
